feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: identify customer posted items SAP HANA S4
entry.content: How to identify my customer posted items which are not cleared yet using SAP HANA S4?

entry.title: Created By User Changed After /SAPAPO/CCR Transfer from APO to ECC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We observed a scenario in our APO–ECC integration landscape where, after executing a repair/transfer activity in transaction /SAPAPO/CCR, the “Created By” user of the process/planned order in ECC (COOISPI) is showing as the user who executed the CCR transfer.

Scenario:

  1. As part of clearing CIF inconsistencies plant-wise through /SAPAPO/CCR, users execute transfer/repair activities without identifying any specific order individually.
  2. After the inconsistencies are corrected and orders are recreated/transferred in ECC, the “Created By” field in COOISPI shows the user who executed the CCR activity.

Questions:

  • Is this standard SAP behavior?
  • Does CCR technically recreate the order using the logged-in user ID?
  • Is it possible to retain the original creator or RFC user instead of the CCR execution user?
  • We also observed that this behavior is happening only for Sales Order related planned/process orders which are recreated or converted by the CCR execution user. Other SFG level material orders are not showing this behavior. Is there any specific reason for this difference?

Environment:
APO PPDS integrated with ECC Process Orders.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: I need to handle vendor batch traceability and separate batches per arrival in SAP S/4HANA.
entry.content: Our main concern is maintaining traceability of vendor batches, creating a separate batch for each vendor batch respecting the expiration date. We want to record stock in transit and its value upon shipping from the origin, and also record the invoice before goods arrive, for better planning and payments. Previously, with movement 107 in older SAP versions, we could generate stock in transit without creating a batch, and then create batches upon arrival with movement 109 that match vendor batches and expiration dates. In SAP S/4HANA, movement 107 generates a batch that we can't modify upon arrival, causing all imported goods to be recorded in a single batch instead of multiple vendor batches. How can we follow the same process to respect each vendor batch and expiration date in SAP S/4HANA?

entry.title: the memory drop down box appears but you are no longer able to click on it and use it.
entry.content: In multiple transactions, when the history / memory drop down appears when typing in a field, your history is appearing but you cannot select it. You click on it and it does nothing. We use to be able to click on it and it would take it into the box.

entry.title: DBCOを使用したOracle への接続について
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

 DBCOを使用したOracle への接続について確認させてください。

 【対象】 S/4HANA 2022 / 2023 / 2025

 【質問】
上記対象からDBCO接続可能なOracleバージョンを確認したいです。

・技術的に接続可能な構成は存在するか
・SAPサポート対象か
・サポート対象外の場合、制限事項は何か 

参考となる資料などありましたらご教授いただけますでしょうか。

 

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Hana DB Audit Policy
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We would like to know about the HANA DB audit policy as we want to create policy in our system. Could you please help us to set up the policy as per the guidelines.

Please let us know if there are not SAP Notes/ blogs which we need to follow.


Regards
Mahendra

entry.title: Table names for Process order in sap hana
entry.content:
Hi All,
Currently my team is working on the process order data in sap hana and we are looking certain fields into sap hana.
Thanks for the help .

Raw Target Quantity
Raw Actual Quantity
Actual Run Time
Target Run Time
Input Product
Total Actual Quantity
Total Target Quantity

entry.title: While doing MIRO business got the error negative vaule
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Issue: While posting the MIRO business got the error “moving average price for material be negative” since they have parked to document but while posting got the error below is screenshot.

Business wanted to post the MIRO document, as already posted

entry.title: Using SSO with Hana 2.0 and Azure AD
entry.content:
We have SAP Hana DB 2.00.079 and i want to login XS Classic engine with Azure AD. I created a Azure test account, configure single sign-on with SAML and everything working as expected.

I changed Hana side config/certificates for Azure prod but I couldn't login with prod Azure account. I enabled the traces and I can see below error.
Authentication SAMLAuthenticator.cpp(01122) : %%% [error: 4040007] [correlation id: 0x9e9a3ab65af736fe] Internal error occurred: Invalid principal id for principal .
XSSession XSSessionLifecycle.cpp(00379) : Assertion authentication for user xxx@xxx.com failed with reason: ptime::PtimeException

I am login Azure test account with user/pass. Prod Azure account working with windows session, so it is using X509 login to Azure.

I checked both Prod and Test Azure SAML Responses, key differences like below.

ProdTest
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/x509</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/password</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/unspecified</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-23T06:58:06.566Z" SessionIndex="_ef3f88b7-4876-40c6-b948-0c838ab31c00">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Unspecified</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-19T08:47:16.019Z" SessionIndex="_b98a9868-70a8-4137-a071-eef985d76100">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Password</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>

Hana can read to user NameID from SAML Response but i couldn't match the user. I hope someone can share some clue.

entry.title: RedHat Compatible version for S4HANA 2025 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are planning for S4 HANA upgrade to 2025 from 2022.
Current OS = RHEL 8.10 for both SAP application and DB server.
Current HANA DB = 2.00.79.04

Question:- 
1. We want to upgrade OS to RHEL 9.6. Is RHEL 9.7 supported by SAP for S4 HANA upgrade 2025?
2. If we stick to RHEL 9.6, then what happens to minor OS patching and how to handle monthly bug fixes from RedHat?
3. Do we have to freeze minor patching of RHEL 9.6 until RHEL 9.7 is certified by SAP?

We have referred SAP note 2235581.

Please let us know.

Thanks, Ramesh

entry.title: In Upgrade Operating Concern get deactivate
entry.content:
Dear Sir/Madam,

It has found that when ever we upgrade SAP our operating concern get deactivate.

We have to first go to T-code KEA0 and activate same by generating structure table.

As per my understanding it is basically ORG level setting then why it get de activate in every upgrade.

Can you please tell us reason for same.

 

Regard

Shukla Vinod

entry.title: Does SAP HANA Keep Partition Locations Across Nodes After Import?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am planning to change the schema of a large partitioned table in SAP HANA.

The system is configured in a scale-out environment, and I am considering using the export/import method.

For example, in the 'Products' table:

Partitions 1 and 5 are located on node 1
Partitions 2 and 3 are located on node 2
Partition 4 is located on node 3

In this scenario, is there a way to preserve the partition placement across nodes during the export/import process?

If so, could you please advise on the recommended approach or best practices?

Thank you.

entry.title: Clarification required on Standard delivered hints
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

As described in SAP Note 2700051, we are planning to implement the delivered hints in our landscape. To ensure proper testing and avoid any unexpected issues, we would like to identify the transaction codes that utilize these hints.

Could you please guide us on how to determine the application source as well as the associated transaction codes for these hints?

Regards,  Jana

entry.title: TenantDBにおけるMinicheckのM1143について
entry.content:
SAP PERSON_NAME 2 SPS 08のTenantDBでSQL『HANA_Configuration_MiniChecks_2.00.080+.txt』を実施しました。
実施結果を『OSS_Minicheck.xlsx』として添付しますので、M1143について対応事項を確認させてください。

M1143についてNote.DIGITSを確認したところ詳細に下記の記載がありました。
『特定のアプリケーションソースが大量の SQL キャッシュ (動的文、大量の文、動的 IN リストまたは OR 連結長、バインド変数なしなど) を消費する理由を確認し、SQL キャッシュの使用を減らすために適切なアクションを実行します。』

分析SQL文"HANA_Memory_SQLCache_TopConsumers"を実行結果を『M1143_HANA_Memory_SQLCache_TopConsumers.txt』として添付します。

Minicheckの本警告について、対応が必要かどうかはどのように判断できますでしょうか。
分析SQL文を実行しましたが、対応要否の判断が不明です。
また対応が必要な場合、具体的な対応手順についてご教示いただけますでしょうか。

以上、よろしくお願いします。


--- Support Assistant ---

Q: Which SAP PERSON_NAME Platform Version and revision do you use (eg IP_ADDRESS)?
A: Revision IP_ADDRESS (SPS08) for PERSON_NAME DB 2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Error DD_ABAP_LANG_VERS011: Customizing-Includes are not allowed in ABAP language version 5
entry.content: Hello SAP Community, I am currently working on an enhancement project in our SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise system. I am trying to add a custom field (ZZ001) by including it in the standard structure CI_COBL. However, when I attempt to activate the structure CI_COBL, the system throws the following error: "Customizing-Includes are not allowed in ABAP language version 5" Message No. DD_ABAP_LANG_VERS011 I have verified that my custom data element and domain definitions are correct. It appears that the ABAP language version setting for this object is restricting the use of traditional Customizing Includes. Could anyone advise on the best practice for handling this in S/4HANA 2023 OP? Should I try to change the language version of CI_COBL via the Object Directory Entry (SE03/SE11)? Or is there a recommended alternative approach (e.g., using Append Structures or BAdIs) compatible with Language Version 5? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Display warning message on Service Order Fiori Tile SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement to perform some validation in Service Order during save in S4 Service and then raise a warning message. The message should persist in the document till the time actual data has been corrected. 

Currently, I have written the code in ORDER_SAVE BADI and raised the message. The message is getting displayed on Fiori UI but as soon as I close and reopen the Order, the message gets vanished. As my Order can get created via API as well- I need to persist this message so that whenever the Tile is opened, the message should be displayed on UI. 

Any suggestions on how I can achieve this would be helpful.

Thanks in Advance,

Gayatri

entry.title: SAP partners supporting BACKINT/SPLITINT interface
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to verify if this is true but I am unable to access this note

SAP Note: 83792 - SAP partners supporting BACKINT/SPLITINT interface stating that SAP is not supporting this interface anymore: no SAP support is available for problems, malfunctions, or failures in connection with the use of such external backup or storage solutions.

This is the link AI provided but doesn't work -  https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/83792

entry.title: VAT registration number in billing document
entry.content:
Hi i have an BP customer where VAT number is updated to new one, after change of VAT in BP its still picking old VAT number in new billing documents and printing the old VAT number in pdf too.

All partners are same Ship to, sold to , payer and bill to, payer is getting printed with correct new VAT, but ship to is still picking old VAT in both billing doc and pdf

entry.title: PR Email Notification Issues Incorrect Deadline Date/Time & Missing Decision Note in Rejection Email
entry.content:
We are facing two issues with the standard SAP email templates used for Purchase Requisition (PR) and Purchase Order (PO) approvals and would appreciate your help.

Issue 1: Incorrect Deadline Date and Time in Email Notifications

We have noticed that the approval deadline shown in the email notification is displaying zeros instead of the actual due date and time.

Example:

  • PR created on 07-Jul-2026 at 2:00 PM
  • Approval deadline configured for 2 hours

Based on this, the email should show the deadline as 07-Jul-2026 at 4:00 PM.

However, the deadline field in the email is showing an incorrect value (all zeros) instead of the calculated due date and time.

Has anyone faced a similar issue? Is there any standard configuration or data source that controls this value in the email template?

Issue 2: Decision Note Not Available in PR Rejection Emails

When an approver approves or rejects a PR or PO, SAP provides two fields:

  • Decision Reason
  • Decision Note

For PO rejection emails, both the Decision Reason and Decision Note are included in the email notification.

However, for PR header-level rejection emails, only the Decision Reason is available in the standard email template. The Decision Note entered by the approver is not displayed.

We would like to understand:

  • Where is the Decision Note stored for PR header-level approvals/rejections?
  • Is this information available in any standard CDS view, workflow container, or business object?
  • Has anyone successfully added the Decision Note to the PR rejection email notification?

Any guidance, examples, or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: HANA Storage Issue
entry.content:
I have cleaned up the *** pointer data via transaction BD22 by removing all records from *** . The table volume has decreased from *** million entries to *** million entries.

When checking large tables and disk consumption via transaction DB02, the table size did not shrink immediately (I noticed the table size was reduced the next day).
However, the database disk usage overview still shows *** GB occupied with no reduction.
Is it safe to purge all data from table *** entirely?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
